kept: winner mechanism — depth calibration 3-tier (Quick/Standard/Deep) with explicit line thresholds
technique taxonomy (assumption violation, composition failures, cascade construction, abuse cases)
explicit non-overlap section ("what you don't flag", names sibling reviewers by territory)
anchored confidence rubric (100/75/50/suppress-at-25) with persona-specific anchor examples
modified: voice → David's terse operator tone (imperatives, no persona framing, no 'you are a chaos engineer' preamble)
description → trigger-only, 'Use when' + 5 quoted phrases (was a mechanism summary)
dropped `Write` from allowed-tools — reviewers never write; this canonical's Anti-patterns section calls this out explicitly

the four fixed adversarial techniques (assumption violation / composition failures / cascade construction / abuse cases) generalized into an axis-agnostic 'Axis territory' section — review-dimensional is the pattern for ANY axis, not only the adversarial one; a concrete axis instance (e.g. a future review-reliability) would restate these as its own hunt list
folded review-blind-hunter's '<3 findings → re-examine' heuristic into Behavior step 5
folded review-edge-case-hunter's validate-completeness pass into Behavior step 4

origins.verbatim_copy_companions: _source/compound-engineering--ce-code-review--references/ (9 files: bulk-preview, diff-scope, findings-schema.json, persona-catalog, review-output-template, subagent-template, tracker-defer, validator-template, walkthrough) — read for context, none of their content is kept in the canonical body
kept: Quality Gates section (6-item pre-report gate: actionable findings, no false positives from skimming, calibrated severity, accurate line numbers, respect protected artifacts, no linter-duplicate findings) — condensed into review-dimensional's 4-question pre-report gate in the Output section
modified: 6-item Quality Gates list condensed to 4 questions (cite a real line, name a concrete failure, confirm context was read, defend the severity) — the war-game brief attributed a '4-question gate' to code-review-and-quality, but no such gate exists verbatim in that file; this is the closest real analog on disk and the actual source of the fold (see REPORT.md finding)
set_aside: the entire orchestrator/fan-out machinery (Stage 1-6, mode detection, 15-20 reviewer dispatch, headless/autofix/report-only modes) — review-dimensional is a single specialist, not an orchestrator; delivery-review is the orchestrator and stays untouched
persona catalog and stack-specific reviewer agents

kept: five-axis framework naming (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) — used as the menu of axes a review-dimensional specialist can be instantiated for
severity-labelled feedback taxonomy spirit (Critical/required/Nit/Optional/FYI) — informs the normalized finding severity field (critical/high/medium/low)
modified: five named axes generalized into one `{axis}` parameter — this canonical is the pattern for instantiating any single axis, not a fixed enumeration of five
severity labels renamed to David's critical/high/medium/low vocabulary (was Critical:/Nit:/Optional:/FYI prefix convention)
set_aside: anti-rationalization table ("AI-generated code is probably fine" -> more scrutiny, not less) — read and noted, but not carried into the body; it's orchestrator/reviewer-culture guidance, not this single-axis skill's job
multi-model review pattern (Model A writes, Model B reviews) — out of scope for a single specialist skill
dead-code hygiene ask-before-delete protocol — CQ-lens concern, already owned by David's code-quality lens
change-sizing / change-description guidance — not this skill's axis
